reasoning: RULE B applied: single root cause is constraint failure — the query's core filter 'まだメジャーじゃない' (not-yet-major) was entirely ignored. Tool args passed no keyword to narrow results, so the 500-item dump begins with Yubari melon and Tajima beef, both iconic and contradicting the obscurity intent. intent_understanding drops to 2 (food tool correct but obscurity intent missed); practical_usefulness drops to 2 (agent must unsorted-sift 500 items with no obscurity signal); travel_feasibility stays 4 (area/producer info present, minor caveat no 'where to experience' framing); specificity 4 (each record is detailed but 500 unranked items dilutes effective specificity). Groundedness and factual_accuracy unaffected — MAFF GI records are authoritative.
missing_data: Obscurity signal or keyword filter (e.g. lesser-known, regional-niche keyword) to surface items below mainstream recognition; ideally a 'registration_number > threshold' or category filter excluding flagship GI brands.
improvement_hint: Server should support an 'obscurity_hint' or allow keyword arg to get_local_food; alternatively, the tool description should explicitly note that callers must pass a keyword to avoid 22k-item dumps, so agents know to filter by a niche term (e.g. '郷土料理' or a regional keyword) before calling without prefecture.
